what_it_does: An acceptance script that exercises the S0 ui:// address grammar contract: it parses several canonical and malformed address strings, normalizes capability lists to a bool-object form, validates the corpus addresses.json and the live Suite.UI_REGISTRY each against the union record shape, and checks that the existing /ui_info serializer still serves the v1 shape.
observations: Top-level script with a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts and prints.
Loads design/_system/addresses.json and validates every corpus address with conform_corpus.
Validates the live UI_REGISTRY with conform_live without migrating its string keys.
Tests both well-formed address shapes (region/element, kind/ref, @state variants) and malformed ones.
Tests capability normalization including corpus aliases (driven/driven-read-only -> drivenReadOnly) and rejects unknown capabilities.
Uses a temporary FsStore and discovers nodes from the project nodes/ directory.
Asserts build_ui_info / suite.ui_info() still returns the same count and v1 keys/capabilities.
standards: Explicitly frames S0 as structural grammar + semantic record, deferring live string migration to S1.
Embodies 'fail loud' (rule 4) and 'orphan not fabrication' (rule 8) as tested invariants.
Preserves the existing v1 /ui_info consumer shape while introducing a union record underneath.
conventions: Heavy contract docstring at the top naming the file, stage, and rules.
Numbered sections with ASCII comment dividers.
Global mutable PASS counter incremented by the check helper.
Inline assertion messages include the first few problems on failure.
Imports project modules by inserting ROOT into sys.path.
concerns: Global mutable state PASS is incremented as a side effect of check.
The script is not obviously wired to a test runner (pytest/unittest); it runs as a plain top-level script.
Hard-coded address strings and assumptions about UI_REGISTRY length make it brittle if fixtures change.
Tests rely on external files (addresses.json) and suite internals without visible mocking.
A comment mentions a code:// join 'for S3' but the file does not test that join end-to-end.
notes: This is the S0 contract acceptance suite: it proves one structural ui:// grammar can ingest both corpus and live address forms while keeping the old /ui_info output unchanged.
questions: What exactly are the S1 and S3 stages referenced in comments?
What is the full schema of design/_system/addresses.json and Suite.UI_REGISTRY?
How are conformance problems surfaced beyond the first three shown in failure messages?
Is UI_REGISTRY guaranteed to contain exactly the seven entries the test expects?
What is the intended behavior when an address exists on only one side (orphan reconciliation)?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual: it tests grammar parsing, capability normalization, independent corpus/live conformance, and v1 preservation.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a single address grammar and record shape that both the design corpus and the live runtime can satisfy, while explicitly leaving live string migration and deeper code joins to later stages.
novelty: It treats two non-interoperable address grammars as conforming to one structural string grammar by moving kind/region semantics into per-address records, and it validates each side independently rather than requiring parity.
contribution: A concrete acceptance test that codifies the S0 invariants: structural grammar parsing, loud capability normalization, zero-problem conformance for both corpus and live registries, and preservation of the existing /ui_info v1 output.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test script for the S0 ui:// address grammar contract, verifying parsing, capability normalization, corpus and live conformance, and preservation of the v1 /ui_info shape.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that one structural address grammar and union record shape can accept both corpus element/region addresses and live kind/ref addresses without migrating live strings or breaking existing consumers.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter and prints an ok line for each passing check.
symbols.description: The check function is the primitive test assertion used throughout the script. It asserts the supplied condition with a labeled failure message, increments the global PASS counter when the assertion succeeds, and prints a formatted ok message. It couples test logic with progress reporting and global state bookkeeping.
